title: Ipomoea tubulosa
intro: Ipomoea tubulosa is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family, characterized by its unique, tubular flowers and climbing habit. Traditionally, Ipomoea tubulosa has been used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea tubulosa include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ipomoea tubulosa is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or powder, and is also used in traditional remedies such as infusions and tinctures.
taxonomy: Ipomoea tubulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Ipomoea, which comprises approximately 600 to 700 species of morning glory plants. The Convolvulaceae family is part of the order Solanales and is commonly known as the bindweed or morning glory family. These plants are typically characterized by their twining stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. Through its classification, Ipomoea tubulosa is identified as a distinct species within the Convolvulaceae family.
